morta_nuts2
  • Li & Lee Parametric Model
  • View page source

Li & Lee Parametric Model

LiLee — Object-oriented architecture

Multi-population mortality models based on the Li-Lee family.

Class hierarchy:

LiLee                              ← general base class
├── LiLee.Parametric               ← parametric variants (B-splines + P-splines)
│   ├── LiLee.Parametric.FullModel ← full Li-Lee model (two time indices)
│   └── LiLee.Parametric.Variant   ← Lee & Li model (single common time index)
└── LiLee.Classic                  ← classic Li-Lee (gradient descent)

Model equations:

\[ \begin{align}\begin{aligned}\text{FullModel:} \quad \ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_{x,g} + \beta_x \cdot \kappa_t + \beta_{x,g} \cdot \kappa_{g,t}\\\text{Variant:} \quad \ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_x + \beta_{x,g} \cdot \kappa_t\end{aligned}\end{align} \]

USAGE:

# Full Li-Lee parametric model
model = LiLee.Parametric.FullModel(degree=3, n_knots=6, lam=0.1)
results = model.fit(alpha_coef_init, beta_coef_init, beta_g_coef_init,
                    kappa_init, kappa_g_init, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)

# Lee & Li parametric model
model = LiLee.Parametric.Variant(n_knots=10, lam=0.1)
results = model.fit(ax_coef_init, bx_coef_init, kappa_init, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)

# Classic Li-Lee model
model = LiLee.Classic(nb_iter=500)
results = model.fit(ax, bx, bx_gr, kappa, kappa_gr, Extg, Dxtg, Muxtg, xv, tv)

Dependencies:

  • scipy.interpolate.BSpline

  • scipy.special.gammaln()

  • numpy

Reference: Hainaut (2025), equation (1) page 3

class morta_nuts2.model.LL_p.llp_class.LiLee[source]

Bases: object

General Li-Lee class.

Groups together:

  • shared mathematical tools (static methods)

  • the two main model families as nested sub-classes:

    • LiLee.Parametric — two parametric variants (B-splines, P-splines)

    • LiLee.Classic — non-parametric gradient descent

Example:

>>> model = LiLee.Parametric.FullModel(degree=3, n_knots=6, lam=0.1, verbose=True)
>>> results = model.fit(alpha0, beta0, beta_g0, kappa0, kappa_g0, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)
static make_penalty_matrix(n_basis, diff_order=2)[source]

Build the P-splines penalty matrix \(D^T D\).

The penalty is used to enforce smoothness on the spline coefficients \(c\) via the penalized log-likelihood:

\[\ell_p(\theta) = \ell(\theta) - \lambda \, c^\top D^\top D \, c\]

where \(D\) is the finite difference matrix of order diff_order applied to an identity of size n_basis.

Parameters:

  • n_basis (int) – Number of spline basis functions.

  • diff_order (int) – Order of the finite difference operator. 1 penalizes slope changes; 2 penalizes curvature.

Returns:

  • DtD (numpy.ndarray, shape (n_basis, n_basis)) — penalty matrix \(D^\top D\).

  • diag_DtD (numpy.ndarray, shape (n_basis,)) — diagonal of DtD, used for diagonal Hessian preconditioning.

static poisson_lnL(Dxtg, Extg, logmu, logDxtgFact)[source]

Compute the Poisson log-likelihood for the Li-Lee model.

Under the assumption \(D_{x,t}^{(g)} \sim \text{Poisson}(E_{x,t}^{(g)} \, \mu_{x,t}^{(g)})\), the log-likelihood is:

\[\ell(\theta) = \sum_{x,t,g} \Bigl[ D_{x,t}^{(g)} \ln \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} - E_{x,t}^{(g)} \, \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} + D_{x,t}^{(g)} \ln E_{x,t}^{(g)} - \ln D_{x,t}^{(g)}! \Bigr]\]
Parameters:

  • Dxtg (numpy.ndarray) – Observed death counts, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • Extg (numpy.ndarray) – Exposures, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • logmu (numpy.ndarray) – Log mortality rates \(\ln \mu_{x,t}^{(g)}\), same shape as Dxtg.

  • logDxtgFact (numpy.ndarray) – Pre-computed \(\ln(D_{x,t,g}!)\) via scipy.special.gammaln.

Returns:

  • lnL (float) — scalar log-likelihood \(\ell(\theta)\).

  • exp_logmu (numpy.ndarray) — \(\mu_{x,t}^{(g)} = \exp(\ln \mu)\).

  • weighted_exp (numpy.ndarray) — expected deaths \(E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)}\).

  • residual (numpy.ndarray) — Pearson residuals \(D_{x,t}^{(g)} - E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)}\).

static effective_dof_spline_block(B, w_flat, lam, DtD)[source]

Effective degrees of freedom for one B-spline block under P-splines penalty.

For a penalized Poisson GLM, the effective dofs of a single spline block are the trace of its hat-matrix contribution:

\[\text{dof}_\text{eff} = \text{tr}\!\left[ \left(B^T W B + \lambda D^T D\right)^{-1} B^T W B \right]\]

where \(W = \text{diag}(w)\) are the final Poisson weights. When \(\lambda = 0\) this reduces to \(n_{\text{basis}}\) exactly.

Parameters:

  • B (numpy.ndarray) – B-spline basis matrix, shape (nb_ages, n_basis).

  • w_flat (numpy.ndarray) – Aggregated Poisson weights per age, shape (nb_ages,). Typically weighted_exp[:, :, g].sum(axis=1) for region g, or weighted_exp.sum(axis=(1, 2)) for a common block.

  • lam (float) – P-splines penalty weight \(\lambda\).

  • DtD (numpy.ndarray) – Penalty matrix \(D^T D\), shape (n_basis, n_basis).

Returns:

Effective dofs for this block (scalar).

Return type:

float

static compute_fit_stats(Dxtg, Extg, logmu, logDxtgFact, dofs)[source]

Compute Poisson deviance, AIC, and BIC given pre-computed effective dofs.

Poisson deviance

\[\mathcal{D} = 2\bigl(\ell_{\text{sat}} - \ell(\hat{\theta})\bigr)\]

where the saturated log-likelihood is:

\[\ell_{\text{sat}} = \sum_{x,t,g} \left[ D_{x,t}^{(g)} \ln\!\frac{D_{x,t}^{(g)}}{E_{x,t}^{(g)}} - D_{x,t}^{(g)} \right]\]

Information criteria

\[\text{AIC} = 2 \cdot \text{dofs} - 2\,\ell(\hat{\theta}), \qquad \text{BIC} = \text{dofs} \cdot \ln(N) - 2\,\ell(\hat{\theta})\]

The effective dofs must be computed by the calling fit() method using LiLee.effective_dof_spline_block(), so that the P-splines penalty is properly accounted for.

Parameters:

  • Dxtg (numpy.ndarray) – Death counts, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • Extg (numpy.ndarray) – Exposures, same shape as Dxtg.

  • logmu (numpy.ndarray) – Fitted log mortality rates, same shape as Dxtg.

  • logDxtgFact (numpy.ndarray) – Pre-computed \(\ln(D!)\), same shape as Dxtg.

  • dofs (float) – Effective number of parameters (float).

Returns:

DataFrame with columns ["N", "dofs", "lnL", "deviance", "AIC", "BIC"].

Return type:

pandas.DataFrame

static difference_matrix(n, k)[source]

Construct the finite difference matrix of order k for vectors of length n.

The \(k\)-th order finite difference operator applied to a vector \(v \in \mathbb{R}^n\) yields a vector of length \(n - k\) whose \(i\)-th entry is:

\[(\Delta^k v)_i = \sum_{j=0}^{k} (-1)^j \binom{k}{j} \, v_{i+j}\]

The matrix \(D\) of shape \((n-k, n)\) encodes this operation as \(D v = \Delta^k v\).

Parameters:

  • n (int) – Length of the input vector.

  • k (int) – Difference order. Must be strictly less than n.

Returns:

Finite difference matrix of shape (n - k, n).

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

Raises:

ValueError – If k >= n.

class Parametric[source]

Bases: object

Parametric Li-Lee model family — B-splines + P-splines penalty.

This class acts as a namespace grouping two parametric variants:

LiLee.Parametric.FullModel

Full Li-Lee model with region-specific baselines \(\alpha_{x,g}\), a common sensitivity \(\beta_x\), and regional deviations \(\beta_{x,g}\):

\[\ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_{x,g} + \beta_x \cdot \kappa_t + \beta_{x,g} \cdot \kappa_{g,t}\]
LiLee.Parametric.Variant

Simplified Li & Lee model with a common baseline \(\alpha_x\) and region-specific sensitivities \(\beta_{x,g}\) driven by a single time index:

\[\ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_x + \beta_{x,g} \cdot \kappa_t\]

Note

Both variants share the same B-spline basis construction and P-splines roughness penalty. All age curves are smooth by construction.

class FullModel(degree=3, n_knots=6, xmin=None, xmax=None, lam=0.0, diff_order=2, nb_iter=800, eta0=0.3, tol=0.001, verbose=False)[source]

Bases: object

Full parametric Li-Lee model.

\[\ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_{x,g} + \beta_x \cdot \kappa_t + \beta_{x,g} \cdot \kappa_{g,t}\]

where:

  • \(\alpha_{x,g}\) — region-specific age baseline (B-splines)

  • \(\beta_x\) — common age sensitivity (B-splines)

  • \(\kappa_t\) — common time index

  • \(\beta_{x,g}\) — regional age sensitivity (B-splines)

  • \(\kappa_{g,t}\) — regional time index

Parameters:

  • degree (int) – B-spline degree (3 recommended).

  • n_knots (int) – Number of internal knots (6 recommended).

  • xmin (float or None) – Minimum age bound (inferred from data if None).

  • xmax (float or None) – Maximum age bound (inferred from data if None).

  • lam (float) – P-splines penalty weight.

  • diff_order (int) – Difference order for the penalty matrix (2 recommended).

  • nb_iter (int) – Maximum number of NR iterations.

  • eta0 (float) – Initial learning rate (0.30 recommended).

  • tol (float) – Convergence tolerance.

  • verbose (bool) – Whether to display iteration progress.

static compute_logmu_lilee(alpha_coef, beta_coef, beta_g_coef, kappa, kappa_g, xv, B, knots, degree)[source]

Reconstruct \(\ln(\mu_{x,t,g})\) for the full Li-Lee model:

\[\ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_{x,g} + \beta_x \cdot \kappa_t + \beta_{x,g} \cdot \kappa_{g,t}\]
Parameters:

  • alpha_coef (numpy.ndarray) – B-spline coefficients of \(\alpha_{x,g}\), shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

  • beta_coef (numpy.ndarray) – B-spline coefficients of \(\beta_x\), shape (n_basis,).

  • beta_g_coef (numpy.ndarray) – B-spline coefficients of \(\beta_{x,g}\), shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

  • kappa (numpy.ndarray) – Common time index \(\kappa_t\), shape (nb_years,).

  • kappa_g (numpy.ndarray) – Regional time indices \(\kappa_{g,t}\), shape (nb_regions, nb_years).

  • xv (numpy.ndarray) – Age vector, shape (nb_ages,).

  • B (numpy.ndarray) – Pre-computed B-spline basis matrix, shape (nb_ages, n_basis).

  • knots (numpy.ndarray) – Full knot vector.

  • degree (int) – B-spline degree.

Returns:

  • logmu (numpy.ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions))

  • alpha (numpy.ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_regions)) — evaluated \(\alpha_{x,g}\)

  • beta (numpy.ndarray, shape (nb_ages,)) — evaluated \(\beta_x\)

  • beta_g (numpy.ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_regions)) — evaluated \(\beta_{x,g}\)

static update_alpha_coef(alpha_coef, B, residual, weighted_exp, eta, lam, DtD, diag_DtD)[source]

Newton-Raphson update of \(\alpha_{x,g}\) (region-specific baseline, one coefficient vector per region).

For each region \(g\) and basis function \(j\):

\[\text{num}_{j,g} = \sum_{x,t} r_{x,t}^{(g)} B_j(x) - 2\lambda (D^\top D \, c^{\alpha}_g)_j\]
\[\text{den}_{j,g} = \sum_{x,t} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} B_j(x)^2 + 2\lambda (D^\top D)_{jj}\]
\[c^{\alpha}_{g,j} \leftarrow c^{\alpha}_{g,j} + \eta \, \frac{\text{num}_{j,g}}{\text{den}_{j,g}}\]
Parameters:

  • alpha_coef (numpy.ndarray) – Current coefficients, shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

  • B (numpy.ndarray) – B-spline basis matrix, shape (nb_ages, n_basis).

  • residual (numpy.ndarray) – Residuals \(D - E\mu\), shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • weighted_exp (numpy.ndarray) – Expected deaths \(E\mu\), same shape as residual.

  • eta (float) – Learning rate.

  • lam (float) – P-spline penalty \(\lambda\).

  • DtD (numpy.ndarray) – Penalty matrix \(D^\top D\), shape (n_basis, n_basis).

  • diag_DtD (numpy.ndarray) – Diagonal of DtD, shape (n_basis,).

Returns:

Updated alpha_coef, shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

static update_beta_coef(beta_coef, B, kappa, residual, weighted_exp, eta, lam, DtD, diag_DtD)[source]

Newton-Raphson update of \(\beta_x\) (common sensitivity, a single coefficient vector for all regions).

The gradient is summed over all regions \(g\):

\[\text{num}_j = \sum_{x,t,g} r_{x,t}^{(g)} B_j(x) \kappa_t - 2\lambda (D^\top D \, c^{\beta})_j\]
\[\text{den}_j = \sum_{x,t,g} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \bigl(B_j(x) \kappa_t\bigr)^2 + 2\lambda (D^\top D)_{jj}\]
\[c^{\beta}_j \leftarrow c^{\beta}_j + \eta \, \frac{\text{num}_j}{\text{den}_j}\]
Parameters:

  • beta_coef (numpy.ndarray) – Current coefficients, shape (n_basis,).

  • B (numpy.ndarray) – B-spline basis matrix, shape (nb_ages, n_basis).

  • kappa (numpy.ndarray) – Common time index \(\kappa_t\), shape (nb_years,).

  • residual (numpy.ndarray) – Residuals \(D - E\mu\), shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • weighted_exp (numpy.ndarray) – Expected deaths \(E\mu\), same shape as residual.

  • eta (float) – Learning rate.

  • lam (float) – P-spline penalty \(\lambda\).

  • DtD (numpy.ndarray) – Penalty matrix \(D^\top D\), shape (n_basis, n_basis).

  • diag_DtD (numpy.ndarray) – Diagonal of DtD, shape (n_basis,).

Returns:

Updated beta_coef, shape (n_basis,).

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

static update_beta_g_coef(beta_g_coef, B, kappa_g, residual, weighted_exp, eta, lam, DtD, diag_DtD)[source]

Newton-Raphson update of \(\beta_{x,g}\) (regional sensitivity, one coefficient vector per region).

For each region \(g\) and basis function \(j\):

\[\text{num}_{j,g} = \sum_{x,t} r_{x,t}^{(g)} B_j(x) \kappa_{g,t} - 2\lambda (D^\top D \, c^{\beta_g}_g)_j\]
\[\text{den}_{j,g} = \sum_{x,t} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \bigl(B_j(x) \kappa_{g,t}\bigr)^2 + 2\lambda (D^\top D)_{jj}\]
\[c^{\beta_g}_{g,j} \leftarrow c^{\beta_g}_{g,j} + \eta \, \frac{\text{num}_{j,g}}{\text{den}_{j,g}}\]
Parameters:

  • beta_g_coef (numpy.ndarray) – Current coefficients, shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

  • B (numpy.ndarray) – B-spline basis matrix, shape (nb_ages, n_basis).

  • kappa_g (numpy.ndarray) – Regional time indices \(\kappa_{g,t}\), shape (nb_regions, nb_years).

  • residual (numpy.ndarray) – Residuals \(D - E\mu\), shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • weighted_exp (numpy.ndarray) – Expected deaths \(E\mu\), same shape as residual.

  • eta (float) – Learning rate.

  • lam (float) – P-spline penalty \(\lambda\).

  • DtD (numpy.ndarray) – Penalty matrix \(D^\top D\), shape (n_basis, n_basis).

  • diag_DtD (numpy.ndarray) – Diagonal of DtD, shape (n_basis,).

Returns:

Updated beta_g_coef, shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

static update_kappa(kappa, beta, residual, weighted_exp, eta)[source]

Newton-Raphson update of \(\kappa_t\) (common time factor).

The score and Fisher information, summed over all ages and regions:

\[\text{num}_t = \sum_{x,g} r_{x,t}^{(g)} \beta_x\]
\[\text{den}_t = \sum_{x,g} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \beta_x^2\]
\[\kappa_t \leftarrow \kappa_t + \eta \, \frac{\text{num}_t}{\text{den}_t}\]
Parameters:

  • kappa (numpy.ndarray) – Current time index, shape (nb_years,).

  • beta (numpy.ndarray) – Evaluated common sensitivity \(\beta_x\), shape (nb_ages,).

  • residual (numpy.ndarray) – Residuals \(D - E\mu\), shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • weighted_exp (numpy.ndarray) – Expected deaths \(E\mu\), same shape as residual.

  • eta (float) – Learning rate.

Returns:

Updated \(\kappa_t\), shape (nb_years,).

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

static update_kappa_g(kappa_g, beta_g, residual, weighted_exp, eta)[source]

Newton-Raphson update of \(\kappa_{g,t}\) (regional time factors).

For each region \(g\) and time \(t\):

\[\text{num}_{g,t} = \sum_x r_{x,t}^{(g)} \beta_{x,g}\]
\[\text{den}_{g,t} = \sum_x E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \beta_{x,g}^2\]
\[\kappa_{g,t} \leftarrow \kappa_{g,t} + \eta \, \frac{\text{num}_{g,t}}{\text{den}_{g,t}}\]
Parameters:

  • kappa_g (numpy.ndarray) – Current regional time indices, shape (nb_regions, nb_years).

  • beta_g (numpy.ndarray) – Evaluated regional sensitivity \(\beta_{x,g}\), shape (nb_ages, nb_regions).

  • residual (numpy.ndarray) – Residuals \(D - E\mu\), shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • weighted_exp (numpy.ndarray) – Expected deaths \(E\mu\), same shape as residual.

  • eta (float) – Learning rate.

Returns:

Updated \(\kappa_{g,t}\), shape (nb_regions, nb_years).

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

static normalize_lilee(alpha_coef, beta_coef, beta_g_coef, kappa, kappa_g, B)[source]

Enforce Li-Lee identifiability constraints.

Three constraints are applied to ensure the model is identified:

1. Common factor scale — \(\sum_x \beta_x = 1\)

\[s = \sum_x \beta_x, \quad \beta_x \leftarrow \frac{\beta_x}{s}, \quad \kappa_t \leftarrow s \, \kappa_t\]

2. Regional factor scale — \(\sum_x \beta_{x,g} = 1 \; \forall g\)

\[s_g = \sum_x \beta_{x,g}, \quad \beta_{x,g} \leftarrow \frac{\beta_{x,g}}{s_g}, \quad \kappa_{g,t} \leftarrow s_g \, \kappa_{g,t}\]

3. Common index centering — \(\overline{\kappa}_t = 0\)

\[\bar{\kappa} = \frac{1}{T}\sum_t \kappa_t, \quad \kappa_t \leftarrow \kappa_t - \bar{\kappa}, \quad \alpha_{x,g} \leftarrow \alpha_{x,g} + \beta_x \, \bar{\kappa}\]

so that \(\ln \mu_{x,t,g}\) remains invariant.

init_params(Dxtg, Extg, xv)[source]

Robust initialisation for the parametric Li-Lee model.

Strategy:

  1. Aggregate data across regions → fit a common Lee-Carter (SVD) to extract \(\alpha_{\text{common}}\), \(\beta_x\), \(\kappa_t\).

  2. For each region, compute the residual log-rate after removing the common component, then apply SVD to extract \(\beta_{x,g}\), \(\kappa_{g,t}\).

  3. Project all age curves onto the B-spline basis via least squares.

Parameters:

  • Dxtg (numpy.ndarray) – Death counts, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • Extg (numpy.ndarray) – Exposures, same shape as Dxtg.

  • xv (numpy.ndarray) – Age vector, shape (nb_ages,).

Returns:

  • alpha_coef (numpy.ndarray, shape (nb_regions, n_basis))

  • beta_coef (numpy.ndarray, shape (n_basis,))

  • beta_g_coef (numpy.ndarray, shape (nb_regions, n_basis))

  • kappa (numpy.ndarray, shape (nb_years,))

  • kappa_g (numpy.ndarray, shape (nb_regions, nb_years))

fit(alpha_coef_init, beta_coef_init, beta_g_coef_init, kappa_init, kappa_g_init, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)[source]

Calibrate the full parametric Li-Lee model:

\[\ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_{x,g} + \beta_x \cdot \kappa_t + \beta_{x,g} \cdot \kappa_{g,t}\]
Parameters:

  • alpha_coef_init (numpy.ndarray) – Initial coefficients of \(\alpha_{x,g}\), shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

  • beta_coef_init (numpy.ndarray) – Initial coefficients of \(\beta_x\), shape (n_basis,).

  • beta_g_coef_init (numpy.ndarray) – Initial coefficients of \(\beta_{x,g}\), shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

  • kappa_init (numpy.ndarray) – Initial common time index, shape (nb_years,).

  • kappa_g_init (numpy.ndarray) – Initial regional time indices, shape (nb_regions, nb_years).

  • Extg (numpy.ndarray) – Exposures, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • Dxtg (numpy.ndarray) – Death counts, same shape as Extg.

  • xv (numpy.ndarray) – Age vector, shape (nb_ages,).

  • tv (numpy.ndarray) – Year vector, shape (nb_years,).

Returns:

Dictionary with keys:

  • "parameters" — alpha_coef, beta_coef, beta_g_coef, kappa, kappa_g

  • "curves" — alpha_xg, beta_x, beta_xg

  • "fitted_values" — log_mu, mu

  • "fit_statistics" — pandas.DataFrame

Return type:

dict

Raises:

ValueError – If the shapes of the initial coefficient arrays are inconsistent with the computed B-spline basis.

class Variant(degree=2, n_knots=10, xmin=None, xmax=None, lam=0.0, diff_order=2, nb_iter=800, eta0=0.2, tol=0.001, verbose=False)[source]

Bases: object

Simplified Lee & Li parametric model — single common time index.

\[\ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_x + \beta_{x,g} \cdot \kappa_t\]

where:

  • \(\alpha_x\) — common age baseline (no region dimension)

  • \(\beta_{x,g}\) — region-specific age sensitivity (one B-spline curve per region \(g\))

  • \(\kappa_t\) — single common time index

Note

This variant was previously implemented as LeeCarter.Parametric.Multiregion in lcp_class.py.

Parameters:

  • degree (int) – B-spline degree.

  • n_knots (int) – Number of internal knots.

  • xmin (float or None) – Minimum age bound (inferred from data if None).

  • xmax (float or None) – Maximum age bound (inferred from data if None).

  • lam (float) – P-splines penalty parameter.

  • diff_order (int) – Order of the penalty difference operator.

  • nb_iter (int) – Maximum number of iterations.

  • eta0 (float) – Initial learning rate.

  • tol (float) – Convergence tolerance on the log-likelihood increment.

  • verbose (bool) – Whether to print iteration progress.

static compute_logmu(ax_coef, bx_coef, kappa, xv, B, knots, degree)[source]

Reconstruct \(\ln(\mu_{x,t,g})\) for the Lee & Li model:

\[\ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_x + \beta_{x,g} \cdot \kappa_t\]
Parameters:

  • ax_coef (numpy.ndarray) – B-spline coefficients of \(\alpha_x\), shape (n_basis,).

  • bx_coef (numpy.ndarray) – B-spline coefficients of \(\beta_{x,g}\), shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

  • kappa (numpy.ndarray) – Common time index \(\kappa_t\), shape (nb_years,).

  • xv (numpy.ndarray) – Age vector, shape (nb_ages,).

  • B (numpy.ndarray) – Pre-computed B-spline basis matrix, shape (nb_ages, n_basis).

  • knots (numpy.ndarray) – Full knot vector.

  • degree (int) – B-spline degree.

Returns:

  • logmu (numpy.ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions))

  • ax (numpy.ndarray, shape (nb_ages,)) — evaluated \(\alpha_x\)

  • bx_reg (numpy.ndarray, shape (nb_ages, nb_regions)) — evaluated \(\beta_{x,g}\)

static update_bx_coef(bx_coef, B, kappa, residual, weighted_exp, eta, lam, DtD, diag_DtD)[source]

Newton-Raphson update of \(\beta_{x,g}\) (one B-spline curve per region).

For each region \(g\) and basis function \(j\):

\[\text{num}_{j,g} = \sum_{x,t} r_{x,t}^{(g)} B_j(x) \kappa_t - 2\lambda (D^\top D \, c^{\beta_g}_g)_j\]
\[\text{den}_{j,g} = \sum_{x,t} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \bigl(B_j(x) \kappa_t\bigr)^2 + 2\lambda (D^\top D)_{jj}\]
\[c^{\beta_g}_{g,j} \leftarrow c^{\beta_g}_{g,j} + \eta \, \frac{\text{num}_{j,g}}{\text{den}_{j,g}}\]
Parameters:

  • bx_coef (numpy.ndarray) – Current coefficients, shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

  • B (numpy.ndarray) – B-spline basis matrix, shape (nb_ages, n_basis).

  • kappa (numpy.ndarray) – Common time index \(\kappa_t\), shape (nb_years,).

  • residual (numpy.ndarray) – Residuals \(D - E\mu\), shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • weighted_exp (numpy.ndarray) – Expected deaths \(E\mu\), same shape as residual.

  • eta (float) – Learning rate.

  • lam (float) – P-spline penalty \(\lambda\).

  • DtD (numpy.ndarray) – Penalty matrix \(D^\top D\), shape (n_basis, n_basis).

  • diag_DtD (numpy.ndarray) – Diagonal of DtD, shape (n_basis,).

Returns:

Updated bx_coef, shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

static update_ax_coef(ax_coef, B, residual, weighted_exp, eta, lam, DtD, diag_DtD)[source]

Newton-Raphson update of the B-spline coefficients of \(\alpha_x\).

Since \(\alpha_x\) is common to all regions, the gradient is summed over all \(g\):

\[\text{num}_j = \sum_{x,t,g} r_{x,t}^{(g)} B_j(x) - 2\lambda (D^\top D \, c^{\alpha})_j\]
\[\text{den}_j = \sum_{x,t,g} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} B_j(x)^2 + 2\lambda (D^\top D)_{jj}\]
\[c^{\alpha}_j \leftarrow c^{\alpha}_j + \eta \, \frac{\text{num}_j}{\text{den}_j}\]
Parameters:

  • ax_coef (numpy.ndarray) – Current coefficients, shape (n_basis,).

  • B (numpy.ndarray) – B-spline basis matrix, shape (nb_ages, n_basis).

  • residual (numpy.ndarray) – Residuals \(D - E\mu\), shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • weighted_exp (numpy.ndarray) – Expected deaths \(E\mu\), same shape as residual.

  • eta (float) – Learning rate.

  • lam (float) – P-spline penalty \(\lambda\).

  • DtD (numpy.ndarray) – Penalty matrix \(D^\top D\), shape (n_basis, n_basis).

  • diag_DtD (numpy.ndarray) – Diagonal of DtD, shape (n_basis,).

Returns:

Updated ax_coef, shape (n_basis,).

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

static update_kappa(kappa, bx_reg, residual, weighted_exp, eta)[source]

Newton-Raphson update of the common time index \(\kappa_t\).

For the Lee & Li variant, all regions share \(\kappa_t\), so the gradient is summed over both ages and regions:

\[\text{num}_t = \sum_{x,g} r_{x,t}^{(g)} \beta_{x,g}\]
\[\text{den}_t = \sum_{x,g} E_{x,t}^{(g)} \mu_{x,t}^{(g)} \beta_{x,g}^2\]
\[\kappa_t \leftarrow \kappa_t + \eta \, \frac{\text{num}_t}{\text{den}_t}\]
Parameters:

  • kappa (numpy.ndarray) – Current time index, shape (nb_years,).

  • bx_reg (numpy.ndarray) – Evaluated regional sensitivity \(\beta_{x,g}\), shape (nb_ages, nb_regions).

  • residual (numpy.ndarray) – Residuals \(D - E\mu\), shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • weighted_exp (numpy.ndarray) – Expected deaths \(E\mu\), same shape as residual.

  • eta (float) – Learning rate.

Returns:

Updated \(\kappa_t\), shape (nb_years,).

Return type:

numpy.ndarray

static rescale_bx_kappa(bx_coef, bx_reg, kappa)[source]

Normalize \(\beta_{x,g}\) via the average across regions.

The identifiability constraint enforced is:

\[\sum_x \bar{\beta}_x = 1, \qquad \bar{\beta}_x = \frac{1}{G} \sum_g \beta_{x,g}\]

This is achieved by computing the scale factor \(s = \sum_x \bar{\beta}_x\) and applying:

\[\beta_{x,g} \leftarrow \frac{\beta_{x,g}}{s}, \quad c^{\beta_g} \leftarrow \frac{c^{\beta_g}}{s}, \quad \kappa_t \leftarrow s \, \kappa_t\]

so that the product \(\beta_{x,g} \kappa_t\) is preserved.

Parameters:

  • bx_coef (numpy.ndarray) – Coefficients of \(\beta_{x,g}\), shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

  • bx_reg (numpy.ndarray) – Evaluated \(\beta_{x,g}\), shape (nb_ages, nb_regions).

  • kappa (numpy.ndarray) – Time index, shape (nb_years,).

Returns:

Rescaled (bx_coef, bx_reg, kappa).

Return type:

tuple(numpy.ndarray, numpy.ndarray, numpy.ndarray)

fit(ax_coef_init, bx_coef_init, kappa_init, Extg, Dxtg, xv, tv)[source]

Fit the Lee & Li parametric model:

\[\ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_x + \beta_{x,g} \cdot \kappa_t\]
Parameters:

  • ax_coef_init (numpy.ndarray) – Initial coefficients of \(\alpha_x\), shape (n_basis,).

  • bx_coef_init (numpy.ndarray) – Initial coefficients of \(\beta_{x,g}\), shape (nb_regions, n_basis).

  • kappa_init (numpy.ndarray) – Initial time index \(\kappa_t\), shape (nb_years,).

  • Extg (numpy.ndarray) – Exposures, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • Dxtg (numpy.ndarray) – Death counts, same shape as Extg.

  • xv (numpy.ndarray) – Age vector, shape (nb_ages,).

  • tv (numpy.ndarray) – Year vector, shape (nb_years,).

Returns:

Dictionary with keys:

  • "parameters" — ax_coef, bx_coef, kappa

  • "curves" — alpha_x, beta_xg

  • "fitted_values" — log_mu, mu

  • "fit_statistics" — pandas.DataFrame

Return type:

dict

Raises:

ValueError – If the shapes of the initial coefficient arrays are inconsistent with the computed B-spline basis.

class Classic(nb_iter=500, h=0.0, z=2, verbose=False)[source]

Bases: object

Classic (non-parametric) Li-Lee model fitted by gradient descent.

Step 1 — fits a standard Lee-Carter model (\(\alpha_x\), \(\beta_x\), \(\kappa_t\)) on the data.

Step 2 — fits the regional residual terms (\(\beta_{x,g}\), \(\kappa_{g,t}\)) on top:

\[\ln(\mu_{x,t,g}) = \alpha_x + \beta_x \cdot \kappa_t + \beta_{x,g} \cdot \kappa_{g,t}\]
Parameters:

  • nb_iter (int) – Number of gradient descent iterations.

  • h (float) – P-splines roughness penalty for \(\beta_{x,g}\).

  • z (int) – Difference order for the penalty matrix.

  • verbose (bool) – Whether to display plots during fitting.

fit(ax, bx, bx_gr, kappa, kappa_gr, Extg, Dxtg, Muxtg, xv, tv)[source]

Fit the classic Li-Lee model by two-step gradient descent.

Step 1 — fits a standard Lee-Carter model on the data using _lc_fit() to obtain the common components \((\alpha_x, \beta_x, \kappa_t)\).

Step 2 — fixes the common component and fits the regional residual terms \((\beta_{x,g}, \kappa_{g,t})\) by gradient ascent on the Poisson log-likelihood of:

\[\ln \mu_{x,t,g} = \alpha_x + \beta_x \kappa_t + \beta_{x,g} \kappa_{g,t}\]

At each iteration:

  • \(\beta_{x,g}\) — gradient step followed by normalization \(\sum_x \beta_{x,g} = 1 \; \forall g\), with a P-spline roughness penalty of strength h.

  • \(\kappa_{g,t}\) — gradient step (no centering constraint).

Parameters:

  • ax (numpy.ndarray) – Initial age baseline \(\alpha_x\), shape (nb_ages, 1).

  • bx (numpy.ndarray) – Initial common sensitivity \(\beta_x\), shape (nb_ages, 1).

  • bx_gr (numpy.ndarray) – Initial regional sensitivity \(\beta_{x,g}\), shape (nb_ages, nb_regions).

  • kappa (numpy.ndarray) – Initial common time factor \(\kappa_t\), shape (nb_years,).

  • kappa_gr (numpy.ndarray) – Initial regional time factors \(\kappa_{g,t}\), shape (nb_years, nb_regions).

  • Extg (numpy.ndarray) – Exposures, shape (nb_ages, nb_years, nb_regions).

  • Dxtg (numpy.ndarray) – Death counts, same shape as Extg.

  • Muxtg (numpy.ndarray) – Observed mortality rates, same shape as Extg.

  • xv (numpy.ndarray) – Age vector, shape (nb_ages,).

  • tv (numpy.ndarray) – Year vector, shape (nb_years,).

Returns:

Dictionary with keys "parameters", "fitted_values", "fit_statistics".

Return type:

dict

Previous Next
